user_query:
Build a spaced-repetition English vocabulary deck from the data in input/words.csv and run an initial review using input/review_grades.jsonl.

Please do the following and produce all requested files:
1) Create a persistent vocabulary deck/database and add one flashcard per row in input/words.csv. For each card, include: headword, part of speech, IPA (if present), a one-line definition, up to 3 short meanings, and exactly 3 examples. If a meaning cell is empty, use the definition as meaning 1. Keep exactly three examples even if more text exists. Apply basic tags like "work" and "communication" to all cards.
2) Render each card in a clean learner-friendly text format and append all of them to output/rendered_cards.txt. Separate cards with a line containing eight dashes: "--------".
3) Apply the first review using input/review_grades.jsonl. For each headword and its grade (0–3), record the review outcome and update the card’s schedule. Save one JSON object per line to output/review_application.jsonl. Each object must include at least: headword, grade (as provided), card_id, and the scheduling fields returned after the review (new.interval_days and new.due_at).
4) Export a log of add/update operations to output/add_results.jsonl (one JSON object per line). If a card already exists, update it in place and still include it in the log.
5) Write a concise learner-facing overview to output/summary.md that:
   - Lists each word with its one-line definition
   - Briefly explains the 0–3 grading legend in your words
   - Outlines what will be due next and when at a high level (no exact timestamps needed)

Inputs to read:
- input/words.csv
- input/review_grades.jsonl

Outputs to write:
- output/add_results.jsonl
- output/rendered_cards.txt
- output/review_application.jsonl
- output/summary.md
